cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So the question is about figuring out when Leslie could have gone to the beach based on the times different people saw her doing various things. Let me take it step by step. First, the key points given are: - Leslie woke up at 5am. - Emily saw Leslie in class from 5am to 9am. - Anthony saw her watching a movie from 9am to 11am. - Thomas saw her at the bakery from 11am to 4pm. - Ashley saw her fixing her computer from 4pm to 5pm. - Susan saw her walking to the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. - The beach is closed after 10pm. We need to find the time window when Leslie could have gone to the beach. So the beach is open until 10pm, so she needs to go before that. Also, she can't go during times when she was seen elsewhere. Let me list out all the times she was seen: 1. 5am - 9am: in class 2. 9am - 11am: movie theater 3. 11am - 4pm: bakery 4. 4pm - 5pm: computer store 5. 9pm - 10pm: walking to Statue of Liberty So let's check the timeline: From 5am to 9am: occupied. Can't go to beach. From 9am to 11am: movie. Can't go. From 11am to 4pm: bakery. Still busy. Then 4pm to 5pm: computer store. So after 5pm, what's happening? Let's see. The next activity is at 9pm-10pm walking. So between 5pm and 9pm, what is Leslie doing? There's no mention of her being seen anywhere. So that's a possible window. But we need to check if there's any overlap. Wait, after 5pm, the next activity is at 9pm. So between 5pm and 9pm, she isn't observed doing anything. Therefore, she could have gone to the beach during that time. Also, the beach is closed after 10pm, so the latest she can go is before 10pm. But Susan saw her from 9pm to 10pm walking, so she couldn't have gone to the beach during that time. Looking at the options: A) 5pm to 9pm – this is the time between when she finishes at the computer store (5pm) and before she starts walking (9pm). Since there's no observation during this period, this seems possible. B) 9am to 11am – she was at the movie theater then, so no. C) 9pm to 10pm – she was walking, so no. D) 4pm to 5pm – she was at the computer store, so no. Therefore, the answer should be A) 5pm to 9pm. Wait, but let me double-check. Let me make sure there are no other activities. From
